mnemonics: BITS: Basic Information Transforms Switches - Binary logic uses switches (transistors) to transform information.
keyPoints: Binary logic uses two discrete values: 0 (false/off) and 1 (true/on).
The three fundamental logic operations are AND, OR, and NOT.
Logic gates are the physical building blocks that implement these operations in hardware.
commonMistakes: Confusing the output of an AND gate (only high if ALL inputs are high) with an OR gate (high if ANY input is high).
Forgetting that a NOT gate (inverter) always outputs the opposite of its single input.
